subtasks.description: Retrieve project requirements and relevant tickets from Jira using the Jira API connector from the enterprise tool registry
Query team availability from calendar API (e.g., Google Calendar, Outlook) to identify suitable standup meeting slots with minimal conflicts
Schedule the standup meeting at the identified optimal time slot using calendar API, creating event and generating meeting link
Synthesize project requirements into standup agenda and share via Slack with meeting details, attendees, and relevant Jira tickets

plan.cost_rationale: This workflow explicitly requires multi-tool orchestration across 4 distinct enterprise tools (Jira API, Calendar API, Calendar scheduling, Slack API), making multitool_plan the appropriate specialist for all subtasks. The plan uses 4 subtasks (not fewer) because each step has a distinct purpose and explicit dependencies: requirements must be retrieved first, availability checked second, meeting scheduled third, and agenda only shared after confirmation of meeting details. Tier assignments: small (tiers 1-3) for individual tool calls (Jira retrieval, calendar queries, meeting scheduling) as these are straightforward API invocations; mid (tier 4) for agenda synthesis and Slack sharing because it requires combining outputs from multiple upstream sources (Jira requirements + meeting confirmation) and composing a coherent message. The dependencies ensure sequential execution where calendar checks await requirements context, scheduling awaits availability, and agenda sharing awaits both schedule confirmation and full requirements.
